issue: Landlord refusing to return security deposit
law: State Rent Control Acts; Consumer Protection Act, 2019
sections: Example: Delhi Rent Control Act s.14; Consumer Protection Act s.35 (complaints)
plain_explanation: At the end of tenancy, landlords must return the security deposit minus lawful deductions (damage, unpaid dues). Unjustified retention is unlawful and can be challenged.
steps: Collect rental agreement, deposit receipt, photos/hand‑over note.
Send a written demand with a 15‑day deadline.
If unpaid, complain to Rent Authority or file on e‑Daakhil as consumer deficiency.
Preserve all communication and proof of handover.

issue: Delayed refund for cancelled airline ticket
law: DGCA Civil Aviation Requirements (CAR) Section 3, Series M, Part II; Consumer Protection Act, 2019
sections: DGCA CAR: refund timelines and components; CPA s.2(1)(g) deficiency in service
plain_explanation: Airlines must process refunds within the timelines set by DGCA. Unjustified delay or partial refunds can be pursued before aviation grievance portals and consumer forums.
steps: Keep ticket, cancellation proof and airline correspondence.
Email airline citing DGCA CAR Section 3, Series M, Part II and ask refund timeline.
File on AirSewa if no action, then approach e‑Daakhil for compensation.
Ask bank to reverse gateway charges if service undelivered.

sections: Section 138 – dishonour of cheque; s.142 – cognizance; s.143 – summary trial
plain_explanation: If a cheque is dishonoured due to insufficient funds/stop payment, the payee may proceed under Section 138 after serving a valid demand notice within statutory timelines.
steps: Obtain bank return memo showing reason for dishonour.
Send legal notice within 30 days, demanding payment within 15 days.
If unpaid, file complaint under s.138 NI Act with all proofs.
Keep copies of notice, postal proof and return memo.

issue: Police refusing to register FIR for a cognizable offence
sections: Section 154 CrPC – information in cognizable cases; s.156(3) – Magistrate’s direction to investigate
plain_explanation: Police are duty‑bound to register an FIR for cognizable offences. On refusal, you can approach senior police or seek Magistrate’s direction under s.156(3).
steps: Politely insist on FIR citing s.154 CrPC; request a written refusal if denied.
Write to SP/DCP enclosing complaint and evidence (acknowledgement).
Move an application to the Magistrate under s.156(3) CrPC.
Keep GD entry numbers, emails and acknowledgements.

law: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013 (POSH)
sections: Section 4 – Internal Complaints Committee; Section 9 – complaint within 3 months
plain_explanation: Employers with 10+ employees must have an Internal Complaints Committee. A written complaint can be filed within 3 months of the incident (extendable for reasons).
steps: File complaint to ICC; request interim reliefs if needed.
If no ICC, approach the Local Committee at the District Officer.
In grave offences, also file an FIR (e.g., IPC 354A).

law: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005; Indian Penal Code
sections: PWDVA s.3 – definition of domestic violence; s.17–19 – residence and protection orders; IPC s.498A (cruelty)
plain_explanation: Domestic violence includes physical, emotional, sexual and economic abuse. Survivors can seek immediate protection orders, residence rights and maintenance.
steps: Contact 181 Women Helpline or dial 112/100 in emergencies.
Approach Protection Officer / Magistrate for protection and residence orders.
Consider FIR for cruelty/dowry harassment where applicable.
Seek free legal aid through Legal Services Authorities.

law: Information Technology Act, 2000; Indian Penal Code
sections: IT Act s.66C (identity theft), s.66D (cheating by personation); IPC s.420 (cheating)
plain_explanation: Phishing, UPI fraud or impersonation are punishable cyber offences. Act quickly to preserve evidence and alert banks/police.
steps: Collect screenshots, URLs, headers and bank statements.
Report immediately at the National Cybercrime Portal and dial 1930 for financial frauds.
File local police complaint/FIR and inform your bank to block accounts/cards.
Enable two‑factor authentication and change passwords.

sections: RBI circular DBR.No.Leg.BC.78/09.07.005/2017-18 (06‑Jul‑2017) – shadow reversal within 10 working days
plain_explanation: If you report unauthorized e‑transactions promptly, banks must credit the disputed amount (shadow reversal) within 10 working days, subject to RBI norms on liability.
steps: Report to your bank immediately via official channels and get a complaint number.
Block card/UPI IDs; change credentials; file on cybercrime portal if fraud suspected.
Follow up in writing; escalate to the Banking Ombudsman if unresolved.
Maintain SMS/email alerts and dispute within timelines.

law: Consumer Protection Act, 2019; Consumer Protection (E‑commerce) Rules, 2020
sections: CPA s.2(1)(g) deficiency; E‑commerce Rules – seller obligations and grievance officer
plain_explanation: Online marketplaces must ensure sellers disclose accurate information and resolve complaints. Non‑delivery/defects can be taken to consumer fora.
steps: Raise ticket with platform’s grievance officer; capture responses.
Seek refund/replacement citing E‑commerce Rules obligations.
File complaint on e‑Daakhil with invoices, chats and screenshots.
Use National Consumer Helpline to mediate.

issue: Delayed possession of flat by builder
law: Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016 (RERA); Consumer Protection Act, 2019
sections: RERA s.18 – compensation/interest for delay; s.31 – complaint to Authority
plain_explanation: Homebuyers can claim interest/compensation or exit with refund for delayed possession under RERA. Consumer fora are an additional remedy.
steps: Collect allotment letter, agreement for sale and payment receipts.
File complaint with State RERA Authority seeking interest/refund under s.18.
Alternatively or additionally, file a consumer complaint for deficiency of service.
Track orders and ensure compliance; seek execution if builder defaults.

issue: Bank refused education loan despite eligibility
law: Banking guidelines; Model Education Loan Scheme
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Escalate to bank nodal officer and RBI CMS.

plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Complain to platform and RBI Sachet.

law: IT Act; DPDP Act; RBI digital lending guidelines
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Complain to RBI and Play Store.

issue: Wrong KYC risk rating denial of service
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Ask for written policy and deficiency.
Escalate to nodal officer and RBI CMS.
File consumer complaint if loss caused.

issue: Courier cancelled order but kept convenience fee
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Approach Ombudsman if refused without cause.

plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.

issue: College refused fee refund after withdrawal
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: File consumer complaint for refund + interest.

plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Complain to OMC and DBTL helpline.
Escalate to consumer forum for loss.

plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Seek refund of penalty if wrongful.

issue: Shop refused to accept legal currency
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Report to RBI if bank refusing coins.

issue: Cinema hall not allowing outside baby food/medical needs
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Escalate to manager and file complaint.

law: DGCA CAR on carriage of persons with disability
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Inform bank immediately and lodge FIR.
Seek compensation per RBI locker circular.

plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.

issue: e‑Challan paid but status not updated
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Raise ticket with traffic police/Virtual Court.

issue: Parcel marked delivered but not received
plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Ask for delivery proof and GPS logs.
File consumer case with loss proof.

plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.
steps: Apply on state portal with proofs.

plain_explanation: See cited law/regulator rules for your rights; denial without lawful reason is challengeable as deficiency of service or regulatory breach.

issue: ATM cash not dispensed but account debited
sections: RBI ATM dispute resolution timelines; Ombudsman Scheme
plain_explanation: If cash was not dispensed but debited, raise a dispute quickly. Banks must resolve within prescribed timelines or pay compensation.
steps: Note date/time/ATM ID and keep SMS/balance slip.
Complain to bank within 24–48 hours; obtain ticket number.
Escalate to RBI CMS/Ombudsman if unresolved.
Avoid sharing OTP/PIN; check CCTV request with bank.

issue: UPI payment stuck in pending/reversed state
plain_explanation: UPI transactions that fail or remain pending are auto‑reversed within NPCI timelines; beyond that, raise a complaint.
steps: Note UPI Txn ID and UTR.
Raise dispute in the app (Help > Report) and with bank.
Escalate via NPCI/Bank Ombudsman if not reversed.

sections: CICRA – rights to access and correct information
plain_explanation: You have a right to accurate credit data. Wrong loan entries or delays in updates can be disputed with the bureau and lender.
steps: Raise correction request with bureau and lender.
If not fixed, complain to RBI CMS and escalate to CIC regulator.

plain_explanation: Unfair repudiation or delay by insurers can be challenged before the insurer’s grievance cell and the Ombudsman/consumer fora.
steps: Collect policy, proposal form, claim forms and rejection letter.
Escalate to insurer’s Grievance Redressal Officer.
Complain to IRDAI (IGMS) or approach Insurance Ombudsman.
File consumer case for compensation if unresolved.

issue: Hospital overcharging or denial of cashless
law: Clinical Establishments Act (where applicable); IRDAI circulars
sections: IRDAI cashless norms; State pricing orders
plain_explanation: Network hospitals must honor cashless as per insurer tie‑ups; arbitrary overcharging can be contested.
steps: Call insurer TPA while admitted; get pre‑auth evidence.
Seek itemized bill; compare with package rates.
Complain to IRDAI IGMS/State Health Authority.
File consumer complaint if excess charged.

law: Railways Act; Railway Passengers (Refund of Fares) Rules; IRCTC terms
sections: IRCTC refund timelines and TDR rules
plain_explanation: IRCTC/Indian Railways must process refunds as per rules. Wrongful denials can be appealed.
steps: File TDR within allowed time; keep PNR and TDR ID.
Track refund status on IRCTC; escalate to RailMadad.
Approach consumer forum with proofs if refund denied.

law: Telecom Commercial Communications Customer Preference Regulations (TCCCPR), TRAI
plain_explanation: Telemarketers must respect DND preferences. Report unsolicited commercial communication to your operator and TRAI.
steps: Report UCC by forwarding SMS to 1909/using operator app.
Escalate to PG portal if unresolved.

issue: Mobile number portability (MNP) rejected without cause
law: Telecom Mobile Number Portability Regulations, TRAI
plain_explanation: Operators can’t reject valid MNP requests without specified reasons (e.g., outstanding dues).
steps: Send PORT <number> to 1900 and receive UPC.
Submit UPC to new operator with KYC.
If rejected, ask old operator for reason code and escalate to Appellate Authority.
Complain to TRAI via PG portal.

plain_explanation: Consumers can seek meter testing and provisional billing corrections through the utility’s grievance forum.
steps: Apply for meter testing; record meter readings.
File complaint with the Distribution Company (Discom).
Escalate to Consumer Grievance Redressal Forum (CGRF) and Ombudsman.

plain_explanation: Arbitrary disconnections or abnormal bills can be contested with the utility and consumer forum.
steps: Seek meter test and usage report.
File written grievance to utility; ask for hearing.
Approach Consumer Forum if not resolved.

law: Gas Cylinders Rules; LPG marketing guidelines
plain_explanation: Distributors must ensure correct weight and safe delivery; report short‑weight or leaks immediately.
steps: Weigh cylinder at delivery; note seal and tare weight.
Report to distributor and OMC grievance cell.
Install leak detector and maintain records.

plain_explanation: Undue delays can be escalated to the Passport Officer; RTI can be used to obtain reasons and timelines.
steps: Track status on Passport Seva; raise grievance.
File RTI seeking file movement and pending objections.
Approach CAT/consumer forum depending on facts.

issue: PAN/Aadhaar name or DOB mismatch causing service denial
sections: UIDAI update norms; IT Act PAN correction
plain_explanation: You can update Aadhaar/PAN records and link them; agencies cannot deny essential services solely for mismatch during correction.
steps: Submit UIDAI update with valid PoA/PoI.
Escalate if services denied without alternative KYC.

plain_explanation: Citizens can add, correct or delete entries in electoral rolls through NVSP and BLOs.
steps: Apply online via NVSP with documents.
Track application; respond to BLO verification.
Use cVIGIL for MCC violations during polls.

plain_explanation: Failure to reply within 30 days is deemed refusal; file first appeal and then second appeal to Information Commission.
steps: File First Appeal to departmental Appellate Authority.
If unsatisfied, file Second Appeal to CIC/SIC.
Attach original RTI, proof of submission and grounds.

sections: RERA s.11 obligations of promoter; s.31 complaint
plain_explanation: Promoters must adhere to sanctioned plans and promised amenities; deviations are actionable.
steps: Collect brochure, agreement and sanctioned plan copies.
File before State RERA citing deviations.
Seek compensation/rectification; also file consumer case if needed.

plain_explanation: Housing societies must follow bye‑laws; arbitrary charges can be challenged before the Registrar/consumer fora.
steps: Obtain certified copy of bye‑laws and resolutions.

issue: Employer not paying wages on time
law: Payment of Wages Act, 1936; Code on Wages, 2019 (where notified)
plain_explanation: Employees are entitled to timely wages without unlawful deductions. Claims can be raised before labour authorities or courts.
steps: Keep appointment letter, payslips and bank statements.
Approach Labour Commissioner or file claim under applicable law.

sections: s.4 – eligibility; s.7 – determination and payment
plain_explanation: Gratuity becomes payable after qualifying service or on separation. Delays attract interest and penalties.
steps: Calculate eligibility based on service period.
Apply to employer in prescribed form; seek payment within 30 days.
Complain to Controlling Authority under the Act.

sections: s.5 – maternity leave; crèche facility rules
plain_explanation: Eligible women are entitled to paid maternity leave and benefits; denial is unlawful.
steps: Submit medical certificates and leave application.
File complaint under the Act/consumer forum for benefits.

law: Minimum Wages Act, 1948 / Code on Wages, 2019
plain_explanation: Employers must pay at least notified minimum wages; short‑payment is recoverable with penalty.
steps: Check latest state minimum wage rate.
Maintain attendance, wage slips or witness statements.

law: Factories Act, 1948; Shops and Establishments Acts
plain_explanation: Eligible employees must be paid overtime at prescribed rates for work beyond limits.

law: Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952
plain_explanation: EPF claims should be processed within stipulated time; undue delays can be escalated to EPFO.
steps: File claim via Unified Portal with KYC seeded.
Raise grievance on EPFiGMS with UAN.
Visit RO if needed; keep acknowledgements.
Escalate to CBT member/CPGRAMS if unresolved.

plain_explanation: Insured persons are entitled to medical and cash benefits subject to contributions.
steps: File claim at ESI dispensary/branch office.
Appeal to ESI authorities if denied.
Keep IP number and contribution statements.

issue: Wrong GST charged / denial of GST invoice
law: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017
plain_explanation: Businesses must issue proper tax invoices and not overcharge taxes beyond law.
steps: Ask for correct invoice with GSTIN; verify on GST portal.
Complain to NAA/State GST if profiteering suspected.
Seek refund via seller or consumer forum.

plain_explanation: Delays in processing legitimate refunds attract statutory interest.
steps: Verify return status on e‑Filing portal.
If unresolved, approach jurisdictional AO/Grievance Cell.
Consider Ombudsman or writ in extreme delays.

law: Environment (Protection) Act, 1986; Noise Pollution (Regulation and Control) Rules, 2000
plain_explanation: Use of loudspeakers is regulated by time and decibel limits; violations can be complained to police/PCB.
steps: Record dates/times; capture video decibel app (indicative).
Complain to local police and State Pollution Control Board.
Seek immediate restraint order if persistent.

plain_explanation: Unauthorised constructions can be reported to municipal authorities for inspection and action.
steps: File a written complaint with ward office with photos.
Approach High Court via PIL if civic inaction persists.

sections: Obligations of data fiduciaries; user rights
plain_explanation: Companies must protect personal data and report breaches per DPDP Act; you can seek remedies once rules and Board processes are operational.
steps: Ask data fiduciary for details and remedial steps.
Use grievance redressal specified in privacy notice.
Escalate to the Data Protection Board (when notified).

plain_explanation: Institutions cannot unlawfully withhold original certificates or deny TC after dues settlement.
steps: Write to Principal/Registrar quoting UGC circulars.
File consumer complaint for deficiency of service.

law: State Education Acts; Fee Regulation Rules; CPA, 2019
plain_explanation: Schools cannot collect capitation or unapproved fees; parents can complain to education department and consumer forums.
steps: Complain to District Education Officer/fee regulation committee.

issue: Denial of reservation benefits in admissions
plain_explanation: Eligible children are entitled to reserved seats in private unaided schools under RTE.
steps: Apply within state RTE portal deadlines.
Escalate to District Education Officer if denied.
Consider writ petition for systemic denial.

law: Consumer Protection Act, 2019; Tort law; Clinical Establishments Act (where applicable)
plain_explanation: Negligent medical care causing loss can be compensated via consumer fora or civil suit.
steps: Obtain complete medical records and opinion.

plain_explanation: Victims or legal heirs can claim compensation before the Motor Accident Claims Tribunal.
steps: Collect insurance details and witness info.
File claim before MACT with documents.

law: MV Amendment Act, 2019; Hit and Run Compensation Scheme
plain_explanation: Statutory compensation is available for death or grievous injury in hit‑and‑run cases.
steps: Apply for compensation through the designated scheme.

law: Motor Vehicles Act; State traffic rules
plain_explanation: You can contest wrong challans online before Virtual Courts or traffic police appellate channels.
steps: Check challan details on Parivahan/State portal.
Dispute before Virtual Court or traffic DCP.

sections: IT Act s.66C; Aadhaar regulations on misuse
plain_explanation: Fraudulent use of Aadhaar is punishable. Lock biometrics and report promptly.
steps: Report at cybercrime portal and file police complaint.
Inform linked banks and change KYC.

issue: Builder collected GST but not deposited/refused invoice
plain_explanation: Builders must issue proper GST invoices and cannot pocket taxes.
steps: Request tax invoice and verify GSTIN.
Complain to State GST if tax not deposited.
Raise issue in RERA/consumer forum for refund/penalty.

issue: Bank refusing account closure/charging illegal fees
law: Banking Regulation Act; RBI customer service guidelines
plain_explanation: Banks must allow closure without harassment and disclose charges transparently.
steps: Submit closure request with KYC and zero balance.
Complain via RBI CMS if charged unfairly.

plain_explanation: Carriers are liable for negligence; consumers can seek compensation for loss/damage.
steps: File claim within specified time; keep consignment note.
Approach consumer forum with valuation proof.

issue: Cinema/Events: denial of refund for cancelled show
plain_explanation: Event organizers must refund when shows are cancelled or materially altered.

issue: Airline denied boarding due to overbooking
law: DGCA CAR Section 3, Series M, Part IV
plain_explanation: Passengers denied boarding are entitled to compensation/alternative arrangements under DGCA norms.
steps: Obtain written denial reason and boarding denial document.
Ask for compensation/alternate flight as per CAR.
Complain on AirSewa if not honored.

law: Carriage by Air Act; DGCA CAR
plain_explanation: Airlines are liable up to statutory limits for lost/damaged/delayed baggage.
steps: Keep baggage tags and boarding passes.
Escalate to AirSewa/consumer forum if denied.

sections: Update process and documentary proof norms
plain_explanation: Residents can update demographics/biometrics with valid proof; repeated rejections can be appealed.
steps: Use UIDAI online/offline update with PoA/PoI.
Visit Aadhaar Seva Kendra for biometric updates.
File consumer complaint if service deficiency.

issue: PAN not linked with Aadhaar – service denial
plain_explanation: Certain services require PAN‑Aadhaar linking; resolve linking errors via correction/update.
steps: Update name/DOB in Aadhaar/PAN to match.
Use e‑Filing portal for linking and pay fee if applicable.
Raise grievance if technical error persists.

plain_explanation: Eligible students can seek timely disbursal; delays can be escalated.
steps: Use RTI to find reasons for delay.

plain_explanation: Unreasonable delays in revaluation can be deficiency of service.
steps: Track status and obtain RTI on file movement.
Approach Consumer Forum for compensation if excessive delay.

issue: ATM card captured and charges levied
plain_explanation: If ATM captures card due to terminal fault, charges/penalties should not be levied.
steps: Complain to bank and acquiring bank (if other bank ATM).

law: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016
plain_explanation: PwD candidates have statutory rights in education/employment; denial can be challenged.
steps: Complain to department grievance cell and Chief Commissioner for PwD.

plain_explanation: Unreasonable refusal can be challenged before labour authorities/consumer forum.
steps: Send formal request with last working day proof.
Approach consumer forum for deficiency causing loss.

issue: Bonded labour/withholding wages for notice period
law: Bonded Labour System (Abolition) Act; Contract Act
plain_explanation: Excessive penalties or forced labour practices are unlawful.
steps: Seek legal aid and file claim for unpaid wages.

law: Child and Adolescent Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act, 1986 (amended 2016)
plain_explanation: Employment of children below 14 in any occupation and adolescents in hazardous processes is prohibited.
steps: Seek rehabilitation support for rescued child.

plain_explanation: Demanding/receiving dowry is illegal and harassment is punishable.
steps: File FIR citing IPC 498A and DPA provisions.

plain_explanation: Persistent following/online harassment is a cognizable offence.
steps: Enhance privacy settings and report on platforms.

law: IPC s.499/500; IT Act safe‑harbour rules
plain_explanation: False publications harming reputation may constitute defamation; platforms have takedown processes.
steps: Send takedown notice to platform with URLs.

plain_explanation: Open burning and poor waste handling can be complained to civic bodies/PCB.
Systemic delays can be escalated to bank and RBI.
Pensioners can seek timely disbursal and arrears with interest where applicable.
steps: Complain to bank branch and CPPC.

plain_explanation: Report biometric fraud immediately; banks must follow liability norms and reverse unauthorized debits.
steps: Complain to bank and file cyber report.

plain_explanation: Banks can freeze for KYC non‑compliance but must allow easy reactivation on update.
steps: Ask bank for specific deficiency and timeline.
Escalate to nodal officer/RBI CMS if undue delay.

plain_explanation: Platforms must settle COD refunds per policy; undue delay is actionable.

issue: Cinema overcharging beyond MRP for water/food
plain_explanation: Packaged goods must be sold at MRP; overcharging is actionable.
steps: Take bill and photograph of MRP.
Complain to Controller of Legal Metrology.

plain_explanation: Platform/host policies must be fair; arbitrary refusals can be challenged.
steps: Escalate within platform; cite policy terms.

law: CPA, 2019; Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) guidelines
plain_explanation: False or exaggerated claims are prohibited; consumers can complain to CCPA/consumer fora.
Selling expired medicines is illegal and dangerous; report immediately.

issue: Misuse of personal data by telecom operator
plain_explanation: Unauthorized sharing/marketing without consent can be challenged.
steps: Escalate to TRAI and Data Protection Board (when operational).

plain_explanation: You can appeal assessments and seek corrections/refunds.
steps: Attend hearing and obtain revised bill.
Use RTI for basis of assessment.

issue: Registrar not registering property despite complete documents
plain_explanation: Registrars must record reasons for refusal; you can appeal or seek writ relief.
steps: Request written endorsement stating reason for refusal.
Rectify defects if any and re‑present.
Appeal to Inspector General of Registration.

plain_explanation: Unfair forfeiture without actual loss can be challenged.

issue: Parcel held by customs without reason
plain_explanation: Customs must communicate reasons and allow adjudication; importers can seek provisional release.
steps: Seek reason in writing and assessment memo.
Apply for provisional release and contest levy.

plain_explanation: Airlines must disclose all charges transparently; hidden fees are unfair trade practice.
